item.description: This study explored the role of Glycine betaine (GB) in mitigating atrazine (ATZ)-induced toxicity in soybean and investigated the underlying molecular mechanisms. In this study, GB significantly improved soybean growth under ATZ stress, increased antioxidant enzyme activity, and reduced oxidative damage. Further analysis demonstrated that GB markedly induced the soybean transcription factor GmMYB30 expression, which binds to the promoter of the key BL biosynthesis gene GmCYP85A2 and enhances...

item.title: Clinical audit on antimicrobial stewardship effectiveness in reducing antimicrobial-resistant bacterial colonisation in hospitalised horses
item.description: CONCLUSION: This clinical audit confirms the effectiveness of the implemented ASP and supports its adoption in comparable veterinary settings.

item.title: Clean-Label Trends in Processed Foods: Challenges in Formulation and Consumer Expectations
item.description: The clean-label movement has become a defining trend in the global food industry, reflecting increasing consumer demand for natural, minimally processed, and transparent products. This review examines the key drivers, challenges, and innovations associated with clean-label development, highlighting the interplay between consumer expectations, scientific feasibility, and regulatory compliance. Topics discussed include natural alternatives to synthetic additives, the role of plant-based and...

item.title: Defining Acute Stress Reactions to support large scale combat operations
item.description: The United States military anticipates that future warfare will involve large scale combat operations (LSCO). The expected nature of LSCO will likely increase the occurrence and severity of Acute Stress Reactions (ASRs), responses to extreme stressors characterized by cognitive, emotional, somatic/physical, and behavioral symptoms. Conceptualizations of ASR vary, with ASRs defined as a disorder in the 10^(th) version of the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) and considered a...

item.title: Petioles Occupy a Partly Distinct Functional Spectrum From Laminae in Broadleaved Woody Plants
item.description: The global spectrum of plant form and function has advanced our understanding of ecological strategies, yet the role of petioles has been largely overlooked. Using 7 petiole and 6 lamina traits from 304 broadleaved woody species (2060 individuals) spanning a 4000-km transect in China, we examined the composition of petiole functional structure and its integrated trait space patterns with laminae. Petiole traits define a distinct two-dimensional functional space, structured by orthogonal axes of...

item.title: Comparing Accuracy of Cylindrical and Tapered Implants Using Autonomous Robotic System: A Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: CONCLUSIONS: Considering the study's limitations, both implant shapes were automatically placed with clinically acceptable accuracy. Tapered implants, however, demonstrated statistically superior apical and angular positioning accuracy and a more efficient insertion process without compromising primary stability. These findings warrant further investigation to confirm their clinical relevance.

item.title: Spatial Hearing and Aging Effects on Mandarin Focus Comprehension in Complex Listening Environments
item.description: CONCLUSIONS: PFC serves as a robust cue for focus comprehension even for aging listeners. Crucially, while absolute performance declines with age, the relative benefit of SRM for prosodic processing remains relatively preserved even in complex listening environments. These findings highlight the resilience of certain spatial hearing mechanism in aging, pointing to the importance of spatial cues in designing interventions for age-related communication challenges.

item.title: Feasibility of Elemental and Microstructural Differentiation of Land Snail Eggs from Bradybaena ravida and Cathaica fasciola
item.description: Although species identification is crucial for land-snail eggs, limited effort has been made to identify the species responsible for producing the eggs. In this study, we used laser ablation inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) to measure 54 elements in both the eggshells and adult shells of Bradybaena ravida and Cathaica fasciola and we used scanning electron microscope (SEM) to analyze the microstructure of the eggshells of the two species. Our results reveal that while the...

item.title: A Neuro-Symbolic Bioinformatics Framework for Unlocking Chordate Physiological Dark Data and Validating Allometric Scaling
item.description: Animal functional trait data are essential for macroecology, but massive datasets remain locked in unstructured scientific literature. Traditional manual extraction is inefficient, and general-purpose artificial intelligence (AI) systems struggle with complex biological tables and numerical accuracy. To address this bioinformatics challenge, we propose a multimodal neuro-symbolic framework combining visual-language perception and code-based reasoning. This approach reconstructs complex document...
